---
name: Security Report (Low-Risk Only)
about: Report a non-sensitive security concern. For exploitable vulnerabilities, use private disclosure.
title: "[Security]: "
labels: ["security", "needs-triage"]
assignees: []
---

> **STOP - Read this first.**
>
> **Do not file a public issue for an exploitable vulnerability.** Public issues are visible to everyone.
>
> For sensitive disclosures, use GitHub's private vulnerability reporting on the affected repository
> (Security tab > "Report a vulnerability"), or email security@your-org.example.
>
> Use this template only for low-risk security hygiene items (missing best practices, hardening
> suggestions, dependency notices that are already public, etc.).

## Summary
<!-- Briefly describe the security concern. -->

## Estimated severity
<!-- Informational / Low / Medium -->

## Affected component(s)
<!-- Which repository, service, or component is affected? -->

## Recommendation
<!-- Suggested mitigation or fix, if any. -->

## Additional context
<!-- References, links to public advisories, CVEs, etc. -->

## Confirmation
- [ ] I confirm this report does not contain exploit details for an unpatched vulnerability.
- [ ] I understand that this issue will be publicly visible.
